title: galium pumilum subsp. pyrenaicum
intro: Galium pumilum subsp. pyrenaicum, also known as alpenrose or Pyrenean bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium pumilum plant, a perennial herb native to the Pyrenees mountain range between France and Spain. The medicinal properties of Galium pumilum subsp. pyrenaicum are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential use in treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Galium pumilum subsp. pyrenaicum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
